column133: [Power Issue] ATS Malfunction on site Jerakah
column136:
1. Mode & Controller
1.1 Set ke AUTO
1.2 Reset controller jika hang

2. ATS / Sensing PLN
2.1 Perbaiki sensing voltage ATS
2.2 Ganti relay/komponen ATS jika tidak detect PLN OFF

3. Setting Timer
3.1 Sesuaikan delay start (jangan terlalu lama)
3.2 Pastikan parameter AUTO aktif

4. Battery
4.1 Charge battery
4.2 Ganti jika soak/drop

5. Emergency Stop
5.1 Release/reset E-stop

6. Wiring & Relay Control
6.1 Perbaiki kabel putus/kendor
6.2 Ganti relay start jika tidak bekerja

7. Starter System
7.1 Service dynamo starter
7.2 Ganti jika tidak berfungsi

8. Fuel System
8.1 Isi solar
8.2 Buang angin (bleeding)
8.3 Bersihkan filter

9. Proteksi Genset
9.1 Tambah oli (low oil)
9.2 Cek pendingin (overheat)
9.3 Reset alarm

10. Engine / Mekanikal
10.1 Perbaiki jika mesin seret
10.2 Cek injector/pompa jika tidak hidup

11. Keputusan Akhir
11.1 ATS gagal kirim sinyal → perbaiki/ganti ATS
11.2 Starter gagal → service/ganti starter
11.3 Fuel/engine issue → perbaiki sistem bahan bakar/mesin

column133: Patrol Segment Cicalengka <>Haurpugur Fiber Cable  is dangling,  brushing touching the trees, Slack, lying flat on the ground, Poles and cables are located far from the edge of the railway
column136:
1.  Re-tension the cable using a dead-end grip and suspension clamps on the pole hooks to achieve standard clearance heights.

2. Install a physical kilometer marker or asset tag on the nearest pole for easier future maintenance tracking.

3. Conduct a comprehensive right-of-way (ROW) vegetation clearing within a 3-meter radius of the aerial line.

4. Pull the slack using a cable hoist (come-along) and secure it tightly with automatic dead-end clamps to match required sag specifications.

5. Re-mount the cable to its designated pole fixtures. If the cable jacket is compromised from ground contact, splice in a new section of outdoor-grade armored fiber cable.

6. If accessibility is poor due to heavy terrain, clear a dedicated walk-path for field technicians. If the distance violates regulations, plan a network relocation closer to the authorized utility corridor.

column133: Patrol Segment Karangsari >< Leles Fiber Cable FO cable trapped under the grass, A bamboo tree has fallen onto the cable, Disorganized cabling, A loose cable is dangling close to the ground, The pole is collapsing, Slack cabling
column136:
1. Once the tree is cleared, inspect the cable  for physical damage, tension stress, or punctures.

2. Re-route the fiber optic (FO) cable properly. If it is an aerial cable that dropped, re-tension and secure it back to the poles.

3. Group cables into organized bundles using heavy-duty outdoor UV-resistant zip ties, messenger wires, or cable rings. Remove any abandoned or dead wires to reduce weight load.

4. Lift and temporarily lash the cable higher up to eliminate immediate safety hazards for pedestrians or passing vehicles.

5.Replace the compromised pole completely. If the ground soil has weakened, install a reinforced concrete foundation block or set up temporary guy wires to stabilize it before replacing.

6. Assess if the slack is causing the cable to dip below the minimum clearance height over roads or pathways.

Findings:
- Located outside the railway track near a residential settlement.
- The cable hangs over the railway tracks.
- The utility pole is positioned too close to another pole.
-  The pole and cable run through the trees
-  The fiber optic terminal box is open.
- Lack of street lighting along the fiber optic cable route
- The fiber optic pole is located directly above an active trash burning area.
column133: Patrol Segment Parung Panjang <> Cicayur Fiber Cable Located outside the railway near a residential settlement., pole and cable run through the trees, fiber optic terminal box is open, Lack of street lighting , directly above an active trash burning area.
column136:
1. Secure the low-hanging cable over the railway track and lock the open terminal box.
2. Re-tension or re-hoist the drooping cable to meet international and local railway safety standards (minimum 6.5 to 7.5 meters above the rails)
3. Relocate the pole to establish standard spacing intervals.
4. Clear out all branches, vines, and canopy growth within a 2-meter radius of the lines
5. Lock the distribution box using tamper-proof locks or numbered security zip-seals
6. Deploy Solar Security Lighting such solar LED streetlights directly onto the existing fiber utility poles
7. Clear the encroaching tree branches and mount fire warning signs above the trash burning zone.
8. Install fire-retardant conduits on the burning-zone pole, fix the dual-pole overcrowding,
